block-definition.comment: Put this at the TOP of your drawing script. Snap! paints pen trails as pixels unless it is told to keep them as vectors as well - this switches that on and empties the trail, so what you draw next can be sent to Sangala Studio as lines a machine can follow. (It is the same setting as "Log pen vectors" in Snap!'s settings menu.)

block.l: StageMorph.prototype.enablePenLogging = true; var stage = world.children[0].stage; stage.trailsLog = []; return 'logging';

block-definition.comment: Sends what the turtle drew to Sangala Studio, which puts it on the mat. OPEN SNAP! FROM SANGALA STUDIO - press its Snap! button. The browser will not let a page on the public web call into a program on your own machine, but it does let two windows talk when one opened the other. That is the road this block takes. "JavaScript extensions" must also be ticked in Snap!'s settings menu. THE PEN COLOUR IS THE INSTRUCTION: a BLUE stroke becomes a fold line (perforated); a stroke of any other colour becomes a cut line - or a drawn line, if there is a pen in the tool holder instead of a blade. Nothing moves on its own: a human still presses Make It in Sangala Studio.

block.l: var stage = world.children[0].stage; if (!stage.trailsLog.length) { throw new Error('No pen vectors yet. Put "log pen vectors for Sangala" at the top of your script, then run your drawing.'); } var svg = stage.trailsLogAsPolySVG().src; window.sangala = {done: false, ok: false, why: ''}; // The window that opened this one is Sangala Studio. Two windows may talk across origins; // a page on the public web may NOT call into a program on your own machine (the browser // denies the loopback address space). So hand the drawing to the window. var studio = window.opener; if (studio) { if (!window.sangalaListening) { window.sangalaListening = true; window.addEventListener('message', function (e) { if (e.data && e.data.sangala === 'ok') { window.sangala = {done: true, ok: true, why: 'the Sangala Studio window'}; } }); } studio.postMessage({sangala: 'svg', svg: svg}, '*'); setTimeout(function () { if (!window.sangala.done) { window.sangala = {done: true, ok: false, why: 'the Sangala Studio window did not answer - is it still open?'}; } }, 4000); return 'sending'; } // No opener: Snap! was not launched from Sangala Studio. Try the helper directly, which only // works where the browser allows it (a locally served Snap!). function tryPort(p) { if (p > 8790) { window.sangala = {done: true, ok: false, why: 'Snap! was not opened from Sangala Studio. Press the Snap! button in Sangala Studio and use THAT window'}; return; } fetch('http://127.0.0.1:' + p + '/snap/svg', {method: 'POST', body: svg}).then(function (r) { if (r.status === 200) { window.sangala = {done: true, ok: true, why: 'port ' + p}; } else { tryPort(p + 1); } }).catch(function () { tryPort(p + 1); }); } tryPort(8787); return 'sending';
if (!window.sangala.ok) { throw new Error('Cannot reach Sangala Studio: ' + window.sangala.why + '.'); } return 'sent to ' + window.sangala.why;

block-definition.comment: Asks Sangala Studio for the design on its mat and paints it onto the Snap! stage. Open Snap! from Sangala Studio's Snap! button - a design can only come from the window that opened this one. The graphic appears on the stage. From there Snap! can turn it into a costume, if that is what you want.

block.l: var studio = window.opener; if (!studio) { throw new Error('Snap! was not opened from Sangala Studio. Press the Snap! button in Sangala Studio and use THAT window.'); } window.sangalaIn = {done: false, ok: false, why: ''}; if (!window.sangalaInListening) { window.sangalaInListening = true; window.addEventListener('message', function (e) { var d = e.data; if (!d) { return; } if (d.sangala === 'empty') { window.sangalaIn = {done: true, ok: false, why: 'the Sangala Studio mat is empty'}; return; } if (d.sangala !== 'design' || typeof d.svg !== 'string') { return; } var img = new Image(); img.onload = function () { var stage = world.children[0].stage; var trails = stage.penTrails(); var g = trails.getContext('2d'); g.drawImage(img, (trails.width - img.width) / 2, (trails.height - img.height) / 2); stage.changed(); window.sangalaIn = {done: true, ok: true, why: 'the drawing is on the stage'}; }; img.onerror = function () { window.sangalaIn = {done: true, ok: false, why: 'could not read the design'}; }; img.src = 'data:image/svg+xml;base64,' + btoa(d.svg); }); } studio.postMessage({sangala: 'get'}, '*'); setTimeout(function () { if (!window.sangalaIn.done) { window.sangalaIn = {done: true, ok: false, why: 'Sangala Studio did not answer - is it still open?'}; } }, 4000); return 'requesting';
if (!window.sangalaIn.ok) { throw new Error('Could not get the design from Sangala Studio: ' + window.sangalaIn.why + '.'); } return window.sangalaIn.why;
